集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 17 of 24

Jake and Sam's Treehouse

📖 Reading

326 words · 28 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（17词） Tier 2（4词） Tier 3（7词）

Jake and his best friend Sam decided to start a new project for the summer: building a treehouse in the old oak tree behind Jake's house. It was a big task for two kids, but they were excited to begin.

On the first day, Sam was absent because he had a cold, so Jake studied the plans alone. The plans had come in the mail from Jake's uncle, along with a bag of nails and rope. The old oak was Jake's favorite place in the whole yard, with branches that looked strong enough to hold anything.

When Sam came back the next day, they followed the instructions step by step. It was hard work — they had to push heavy boards up into place and pull each rope tight so nothing would slip. Their neighbor, who was quite rich, kindly lent them a real ladder so they would not have to climb the rough bark.

Still, the ladder felt so tall that Jake was afraid every time he climbed it. Partway up, it began to shake in the wind, and he gripped it firmly, worried he might lose his balance. He told himself that finishing the treehouse was important, and slowly his fear faded.

While Jake worked, he stayed interested in a line of ants marching along a branch — he had always liked watching insects, even tiny ones. From the platform, the yard below looked as small as if he were standing on a mountain.

By the end of the week, the little structure was complete. It even included a small bell that would ring whenever someone reached the top, and a painted flag to represent their clubhouse. Sam said he had never liked bugs, but even he admitted he no longer hated climbing. They now like to sit together on the platform, tired but proud, and the boys could already taste success. They already had plans to improve it with a roof next summer.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "Sam was absent because he had a cold, so Jake studied the plans alone," what does absent mean?
B — Sam could not be there because he was sick, so he was absent.
2In "he gripped it firmly, worried he might lose his balance," what does lose mean?
D — Jake was afraid of losing his balance, meaning he could not keep himself steady.
3In "a painted flag to represent their clubhouse," what does represent mean?
A — the flag was a symbol that stood for their finished clubhouse.
4Why did their neighbor lend Jake and Sam a real ladder?
B — the rich neighbor kindly lent them a ladder so they would not have to climb the rough bark.
5How did Jake's feelings about climbing the ladder change during the story?
A — the story says Jake was afraid every time he climbed, but telling himself the task was important slowly made his fear fade.
🔁
